name: Budget Insights
description: RECOMMENDED APPROACH A — ON-DEMAND IMMUTABLE INSIGHT REPORT COMPILER. On one explicit structured invocation, INSIGHTS binds version-compatible public BUDGET plan/position reads and the minimum public LEDGER time-series evidence discovery proves necessary. It computes a bounded set of deterministic, versioned pace, forecast, trend, anomaly, and attention signals; every signal cites its Analysis Window, As-of Instant, formula/version, source snapshots, evidence sufficiency, and limitations. Unsupported or sparse evidence yields an explicit unavailable or insufficient-evidence state, never invented confidence. The successful result may be retained as an immutable owner-only Report Snapshot; later corrections create a separately cited Restatement rather than rewriting what was shown. INSIGHTS mutates no LEDGER, CLASSIFY, or BUDGET state, runs in the existing self-contained CLI process, starts no daemon, and grants CHANNELS no semantics beyond the released read contract. Complexity M, fits the initial budget, root-problem completeness 9/10, and builds directly on FR-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-PROJECTION, DD-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-READ-PROJECTION, and the existing hard LEDGER/BUDGET dependencies. Pros: explainable, reproducible, local, correction-aware, and channel-neutral. Cons: formula and evidence policies must be explicit, and deterministic v1 signals may be less adaptive than richer models. Biggest risk: sparse or irregular spending makes mathematically valid outputs look more predictive than they are. Stress test: representative owner-only evidence must prove that at least one report shape adds timely, correctly caveated value beyond raw Budget Position inspection; otherwise use the do-less path.

ALTERNATIVE B — CONTINUOUS MATERIALIZED ANALYTICS LEDGER. Replicate transaction, category, plan, and correction events into a separate analytical store, continuously update projections, and schedule report generation. Complexity H, outside the initial M budget, root-problem completeness 10/10, and only partly builds on the explicit-invocation architecture. Pros: fast longitudinal queries, rich time series, and natural scheduled delivery. Cons: duplicates authoritative financial state, introduces synchronization and replay correctness, requires a daemon/scheduler and operational recovery, and couples INSIGHTS prematurely to CHANNELS. Biggest risk: the analytical copy silently drifts from corrected LEDGER/BUDGET truth while producing confident reports. Rejected for v1; reconsider only through a project ADR if measured on-demand performance or a future channel requires it.

ALTERNATIVE C — DO LESS WITH BUDGET OUTPUTS PLUS AN EXTERNAL AGENT OR SPREADSHEET. Publish no INSIGHTS state or formulas; let the owner or AI Agent Host interpret Budget Position results case by case and retain any notes externally. Complexity L, fits the budget, root-problem completeness 4/10, and maximally reuses BUDGET. Pros: no new persistence or analytical semantics and immediate manual flexibility. Cons: formulas, windows, corrections, evidence limits, and retained comparisons vary per invocation; private data may spread into uncontrolled artifacts. Biggest risk: plausible but inconsistent advice becomes less trustworthy than the raw position. Retained as the monitored fallback if a deterministic module does not demonstrate additional value.

SELECTION AND BOUNDARIES. Choose Approach A because it earns durable interpretation without duplicating financial truth or introducing an always-on service. The non-obvious inversion is to treat the report as an immutable evidence artifact, not a dashboard: CHANNELS can later render or deliver it, while INSIGHTS owns only analytical semantics and provenance. LEDGER remains authoritative for canonical transactions, relationships, categories, corrected actual membership, and snapshot identity. CLASSIFY improves category coverage but is not required; Uncategorized Spend stays explicit. BUDGET remains authoritative for Budget Plans, Budget Plan Revisions, exact Budget Positions, and plan mutation. INSIGHTS owns formula versions, evidence sufficiency, Insight Reports, Report Snapshots, Restatements, and attention semantics. CHANNELS owns screens, conversation, scheduling requests, and delivery.

SCOPE SIGNALS AND ROUTING. Signals present: high-impact security/privacy for sensitive financial reports; hard external integrations with BUDGET and LEDGER plus a soft CLASSIFY dependency; a likely analytical model of Report Snapshot, signal, metric, threshold, provenance, and restatement entities; cross-module snapshot flows; and significant unknowns around time windows, forecast validity, evidence sufficiency, correction/restatement semantics, and useful attention thresholds. Five signals are present, with security and significant unknowns independently high-impact. Route to /lex:discovery INSIGHTS before PRD. No new project ADR is required for the selected in-process read-only shape; any future materialized analytical replica, background scheduler, network service, cloud/LLM processing, or mutation authority requires reassessment and likely /lex:adr.
problem_statement: Surface pain: canonical transactions and exact monthly Budget Positions still leave Dirk manually deciding whether spending is on track, what changed, and which categories deserve attention. 5 Whys: Why are insights needed? Raw facts and plan totals require repeated interpretation. Why is repeated interpretation unreliable? Daily, weekly, and monthly comparisons use unstated windows, formulas, and correction timing. Why does BUDGET not answer this? BUDGET deliberately owns exact plan-versus-actual reconciliation and excludes pace, forecasts, trends, anomalies, recommendations, narratives, and retained reports. Why not ask an agent or spreadsheet ad hoc? The answer would be non-reproducible, could hide sparse evidence or corrections, and could not be compared with what was reported earlier. Root problem: Tally lacks a local, provenance-bound analytics layer that converts exact public BUDGET and LEDGER snapshots into reproducible periodic signals, explicitly distinguishes insufficient evidence, and preserves what the owner was shown without mutating financial truth. Without INSIGHTS, Tally remains a trustworthy record and planning tool but not timely decision support. Journey: the sole Personal Finance Owner, directly or through the AI Agent Host, discovers a versioned INSIGHTS operation, requests an explicit period and As-of Instant, receives an explainable Insight Report with exact baseline, pace, forecast, trend, anomaly, and attention evidence or explicit unavailable states, inspects prior Report Snapshots or a correction-based Restatement, and chooses any action outside INSIGHTS.
why_now: LEDGER, CLASSIFY, and BUDGET now define the canonical facts, category-quality boundary, and exact Budget Position contract. BUDGET explicitly publishes a mutation-free read capability for INSIGHTS, so report semantics should be framed before CHANNELS chooses how to deliver them.

rationale: On-track analysis compares actual spend with monthly category budgets.
Classification quality improves category analytics, while uncategorized spend can still be reported explicitly.
Spend analysis is calculated from canonical transactions.
